summary: Good audience recording of a solid but unspectacular show; Jerry’s voice improves while Louie Louie and a few others fall flat.
review: The tape captures the warm, lively atmosphere of the Hartford venue with clear audience sound. Musically the performance is steady – highlights include Bird Song, All Over Now, Masterpiece, Samson and Smokestack – but several numbers (notably Louie Louie, Might As Well and Ship) are described as weak or over‑played.

ai_rating.rationale: Three moderate reviews point to a decent show with a few strong moments but no standout brilliance; the audience recording is praised as warm and listenable, yielding an overall rating just above average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Warm crowd ambience, clear enough for enjoyment; no specific drop‑outs mentioned but the tape is described as a pleasant AUD capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Standard two‑set structure with solid pacing; the second set delivers a few high points but also drifts into predictable or dead‑ended moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice sounds better than previous night but still inconsistent, hot/cold on several numbers and struggling on "Might As Well".
band_member_comments.Phil: Gritty bass work praised, especially on the elevated "Samson" jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals criticized for lack of nuance, yet his delivery adds punch on "Smokestack" and he drives the "Sampson" jam.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's organ work highlighted as a lift on "All Over Now" and "Louie Louie".
band_member_comments.Drums: Percussionists provide furious energy on "Samson"; no major issues noted.
